blurb: These verses whisper that the greatest stewardship is to protect what has been entrusted to us—not talents or treasures, but the simple news of grace. Paul, writing from chains, insists that every prison, every sorrow, every delay in life still finds its center in one task: to keep the mystery unstained, unwatered-down. Let today’s complicated duties shrink into this single calling: hold fast the treasure that another laid in your hands. Guard it, and you guard your own heart.

verses.text: For this cause I Paul, the prisoner of Jesus Christ for you Gentiles,
If ye have heard of the dispensation of the grace of God which is given me to you-ward:
How that by revelation he made known unto me the mystery; as I wrote afore in few words,
Whereby, when ye read, ye may understand my knowledge in the mystery of Christ
